page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-112 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this server features 112 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 56 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading enabled. The hardware configuration allocates 224 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include bundled local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage options.

Benchmark results demonstrate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The AMD EPYC architecture delivers strong memory bandwidth, reaching 7743.98 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 274.99 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic capabilities are highly efficient, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets, as reflected in the top-tier PassMark CPU encryption benchmark. However, single-core performance is moderate, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the middle 50% tier.

The server is highly cost-efficient for parallelized workloads that benefit from high core density without requiring massive memory footprints. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a deliberate tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute throughput over memory capacity. This makes the instance highly suitable for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, physics simulations, and high-throughput web serving, while it is less optimal for memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-112 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 112 dedicated vCPUs and 224 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 56 physical cores and a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is designed for high-density parallel processing. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks highlight top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and strong cryptographic execution, though single-core performance remains average. This resource profile offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is highly suited for software compilation, ray tracing, physics simulations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-112 is a compute-optimized server with 112 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 224 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallelized workloads.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 server delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for parallelized workloads.

Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth
